tool.name: Beacon2 remove dataset
tool.description: remove records for one dataset from MongoDB (one collection or all)

configfiles.configfile:
# Auto-generated by the Galaxy wrapper for Beacon2 CSV to BFF
csv_folder = '.' output_docs_folder = '.' entry_type = '${advanced.entry_type}' only_process_reads_with_allele_frequency = ${advanced.only_process_reads_with_allele_frequency} populations_by_allele_counts = ${advanced.populations_by_allele_counts} reference_genome = '${advanced.reference_genome}' datasetId = '${dataset_id}' case_level_data = ${advanced.case_level_data} exact_heterozygosity = ${advanced.exact_heterozygosity} num_rows = ${advanced.num_rows} verbosity = ${advanced.verbosity}
# Database connection database_host = '${advanced.database.database_host}' database_port = ${advanced.database.database_port} database_name = '${advanced.database.database_name}'
# Credentials (exact same logic as your beacon2-import tool) #set $db_auth_source = $__user__.extra_preferences.get('beacon2_account|db_auth_source', "") #set $db_user = $__user__.extra_preferences.get('beacon2_account|db_user', "") #set $db_password = $__user__.extra_preferences.get('beacon2_account|db_password', "") #if $db_user == "" or $db_password == "" or $db_auth_source == "": #set $db_auth_source = "admin" #set $db_user = "root" #set $db_password = "example" #end if
database_user = '$db_user' database_password = '$db_password' database_auth_source = '$db_auth_source'

tool.help:
**Beacon2 remove dataset**
**⚠️ Destructive operation** — This tool permanently deletes all records belonging to the specified `datasetId`.
You can choose in the **Advanced section**: - `all` → delete the dataset from **every collection** (individuals, biosamples, genomicVariations, cohorts, etc.) - or a specific collection (e.g. `individuals`, `genomicVariations`, `cohorts`, …)
**Use with extreme caution** — this action cannot be undone.
**New features (Advanced section):** - Choose which collection(s) to clean (`all` or specific type) - Full custom `conf.py` support (highest priority) - Verbose logging
MongoDB credentials are read automatically from your Galaxy user preferences.
